theme: History Lecture — The Fall of the Berlin Wall

sentences.english: The fall of the Berlin Wall on November 9th, 1989, is one of those events that has come to symbolize an entire era.
sentences.korean: 1989년 11월 9일 베를린 장벽의 붕괴는 전체 시대를 상징하게 된 사건 중 하나입니다.

sentences.english: But what's often misunderstood is that the Wall didn't fall because of some grand, carefully orchestrated plan.
sentences.korean: 자주 오해받는 건 장벽이 거창하고 치밀한 계획 때문이 아니라, 대체로 관료적 소통 오류 때문에 무너졌다는 것입니다.

sentences.english: It fell, in large part, because of a bureaucratic miscommunication.
sentences.korean: 거창하고 치밀한 계획 때문이 아니라 대체로 관료적 소통 오류 때문이었다는 거예요

sentences.english: To understand what happened, we need some context.
sentences.korean: 무슨 일이 있었는지 이해하려면 맥락이 필요합니다.

sentences.english: By 1989, the Soviet Union was in serious trouble.
sentences.korean: 1989년까지 소련은 심각한 위기에 처해 있었어요.

quiz.question: What does '관료적 오류/소통 실수' mean in English?

theme: History Lecture — The Fall of the Berlin Wall

sentences.english: Mikhail Gorbachev's policies of glasnost and perestroika — openness and restructuring — had unleashed forces that the Communist system couldn't contain.
sentences.korean: 미하일 고르바초프의 글라스노스트(개방)와 페레스트로이카(개혁) 정책은 공산 체제가 억제할 수 없는 세력을 풀어놓았습니다.

sentences.english: Across Eastern Europe, reform movements were gaining momentum.
sentences.korean: 동유럽 전역에서 개혁 운동이 힘을 얻고 있었습니다.

sentences.english: Hungary had opened its border with Austria, allowing thousands of East Germans to flee to the West.
sentences.korean: 헝가리는 오스트리아와의 국경을 열었어요.

sentences.english: Mass protests were erupting in cities like Leipzig and East Berlin.
sentences.korean: 라이프치히와 동베를린 같은 도시에서 대규모 시위가 터지고 있었습니다.

sentences.english: Under mounting pressure, the East German government decided to ease travel restrictions.
sentences.korean: 거세지는 압력 속에 동독 정부는 여행 제한을 완화하기로 했어요.

quiz.question: What does '반체제 운동' mean in English?

theme: History Lecture — The Fall of the Berlin Wall

sentences.english: On the evening of November 9th, a spokesman named Günter Schabowski was handed a press release about the new regulations just minutes before a live press conference.
sentences.korean: 11월 9일 저녁, 귄터 샤보프스키라는 대변인이 생방송 기자회견 불과 몇 분 전에 새 규정에 대한 보도자료를 건네받았습니다.

sentences.english: He hadn't been briefed on the details.
sentences.korean: 그는 세부사항에 대해 브리핑을 받지 못했습니다.

sentences.english: When a journalist asked when the new travel rules would take effect, Schabowski shuffled through his notes, looked confused, and said — and I'm paraphrasing here — "Immediately, as far as I know." Within hours, thousands of East Berliners flooded the Wall's checkpoints.
sentences.korean: 기자가 새 여행 규정이 언제 발효되는지 물었을 때, 샤보프스키는 메모를 뒤적이며 당황한 표정으로 말했습니다 — '내가 아는 한, 즉시.'

sentences.english: The border guards, who had received no instructions about what to do, were overwhelmed.
sentences.korean: 아무 지시도 받지 못한 국경 경비원들은 압도당했습니다.

sentences.english: Rather than risk a violent confrontation, they opened the gates.
sentences.korean: 폭력적 대치를 감수하기보다 문을 열었어요.

quiz.question: What does '기자 회견' mean in English?

theme: History Lecture — The Fall of the Berlin Wall

sentences.english: People streamed across the border.
sentences.korean: 폭력적 대치를 감수하기보다 문을 열었어요

sentences.english: Strangers embraced. Families separated for decades were reunited on the spot.
sentences.korean: 낯선 사람들이 포옹했습니다. 수십 년간 이별했던 가족이 그 자리에서 재회했어요.

sentences.english: And people began physically tearing down the Wall with hammers and picks.
sentences.korean: 사람들은 망치와 곡괭이로 장벽을 직접 허물기 시작했습니다.

sentences.english: Now, it's tempting to view this as a spontaneous explosion of freedom.
sentences.korean: 이것을 자유의 자발적 폭발로 보고 싶은 유혹이 있고, 어떤 면에서 그렇기도 했습니다.

sentences.english: And in some ways it was.
sentences.korean: 보고 싶은 유혹이 있어요 어떤 면에서 그렇기도 했고

quiz.question: What does '지정학적 질서' mean in English?

theme: History Lecture — The Fall of the Berlin Wall

sentences.english: But historians emphasize that the fall of the Wall was the culmination of decades of resistance — dissident movements, economic stagnation, and the slow erosion of ideological legitimacy.
sentences.korean: 하지만 역사학자들은 장벽의 붕괴가 수십 년간의 저항 — 반체제 운동, 경제 침체 — 의 결정체였다고 강조합니다.

sentences.english: The Wall's collapse didn't just change Germany.
sentences.korean: 장벽의 붕괴는 독일만 바꾼 게 아닙니다.

sentences.english: It accelerated the end of the Cold War, led to German reunification within a year, and reshaped the entire geopolitical order of Europe.
sentences.korean: 냉전 종식을 앞당기고 독일 통일로 이어졌습니다.

sentences.english: For better or worse, the world we live in today was born on that November night.
sentences.korean: 좋든 나쁘든, 오늘 우리가 사는 세상은 그 11월 밤에 탄생했습니다.

quiz.question: What does '~에 대해 보고/설명을 받다' mean in English?
